updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.158: Auto Mode Expands to Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry
updates.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code v2.1.158 on May 30, bringing Auto mode to enterprise cloud deployments: Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry now support Auto mode for both Opus 4.7 and Opus 4.8. Auto mode intelligently adjusts Claude's reasoning effort and behavior based on task complexity, a capability previously limited to the first-party Claude API. Developers activate it by setting the environment variable CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1 in their cloud-hosted environments.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog (v2.1.158, dated May 30, 2026). Single-line changelog entry — factual and unambiguous. No speculative claims. Cross-references consistently with surrounding v2.1.154 (May 28, Opus 4.8 launch) and v2.1.157 (May 29, major update) entries.

instagram.hook: Claude Code Auto Mode just went enterprise — now live on AWS, Google Cloud & Azure
slides: Slide 1: Claude Code's smartest feature just landed on every major cloud ☁️
Slide 2: What is Auto Mode? Claude adapts its effort to your task automatically
Slide 3: Now supported: Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry
Slide 4: Works with Opus 4.7 AND the brand-new Opus 4.8
Slide 5: Enable it with one env variable — CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1
